name: Garlic Potatoes
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel the potatoes.
2. Gently boil salted water until they are barely tender, then drain.
3. Return the drained potatoes to the pan and shake over heat until they appear to have a light coating of flour on the outside.
4. Cool and thinly slice the potatoes.
5. Melt butter in a large skillet.
6. Add garlic (pressed) and season with salt and pepper.
7. Add the sliced potatoes to the skillet.
8. Turn the potatoes around in the butter, then press them together to form a large flat cake.
9. Cover with low heat.
10. Turn the whole mass fairly frequently with a spatula until a nice brown crust has formed.
11. Serve hot.
Makes 6 servings.
Food Exchanges per serving: 1 STARCH/BREAD Exchange + 1 FAT Exchange (If using medium potatoes, which would be about half a cup for a starch/bread exchange, and reducing oil by using a pan spray, use 1 FAT Exchange per serving). This is a dish that Bert would enjoy.
